description_long: This radio receiver features a brushed metal front panel with a series of five rotary knobs, a large tuning dial, and several push buttons, all arranged below a horizontal frequency display illuminated in blue. The top and sides are encased in a wood-textured veneer, giving it a classic, vintage appearance. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, and additional connection points, all clearly labeled. The overall build suggests a robust, rectangular form factor typical of mid-to-late 20th-century home audio equipment. The combination of metallic and wood finishes provides both durability and aesthetic appeal.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wood-textured sides, multiple control knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the brushed metal faceplate, five rotary knobs, a large tuning dial, push buttons, and a blue-lit frequency display.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood-textured panel and the metal top with ventilation slits; no controls or ports visible.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, and connection points, all set in a black panel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wood-textured panel and the metal top with ventilation slits; a single knob is visible protruding from the front.
